standing_neutral.name: Standing - Neutral
standing_neutral.visual_cues: Both people standing upright, facing each other, no significant grip or contact. Athletic stance, hands out.
grapplemap_variations: standing vs seated single w/ head outside
instructional_references: Karo Parysian "Judo For MMA" Volume 4: DROP SEOI-NAGE, Section 1 @ 2:06

standing_clinch.name: Clinch / Collar Tie
standing_clinch.visual_cues: Both standing but gripping each other — hands on head, neck, collar, or arms. Heads are close together. Pulling/pushing with upper body contact.

closed_guard_bottom.name: Closed Guard (Bottom)
closed_guard_bottom.visual_cues: Person on their BACK with LEGS WRAPPED around the other person's waist. Ankles are LOCKED behind the top person's back. The top person is between the bottom person's legs, chest-to-chest.
grapplemap_variations: closed guard w/ double under collar
seated closed guard w/ double unders
posture broken full guard w/ hand on mat

half_guard_bottom.name: Half Guard (Bottom)
half_guard_bottom.visual_cues: Person on their back or side with ONE LEG trapped between their legs (not both). One of the top person's legs is controlled/entangled, the other is free. Partial guard retention.
grapplemap_variations: z guard w/ wrist and collar tie
twister pass w/ far arm stuffed
kneeling darce vs half guard w/ underhook

butterfly_guard.name: Butterfly Guard
butterfly_guard.visual_cues: Bottom person is SEATED UPRIGHT (not flat on back) with BOTH FEET hooked inside the top person's thighs. Shins are against the inner thighs. Bottom person often has an underhook.
grapplemap_variations: posture broken seated butterfly w/ double unders
over/under cocoon w/ head on underhook side
knee-pinned butterfly w/ wrist ctrl and overhook
top shooting for underhooks in butterfly

open_guard.name: Open Guard (generic)
open_guard.visual_cues: Bottom person on back or seated with legs NOT wrapped around opponent. Feet are on hips, biceps, or pushing. There is SPACE between the two people. Legs are active but not locked.

de_la_riva.name: De La Riva Guard
de_la_riva.visual_cues: Bottom person has ONE LEG hooked BEHIND the top person's lead leg (from outside to inside, behind the knee). The other foot is on the hip. Often gripping the far ankle.

spider_lasso.name: Spider / Lasso Guard
spider_lasso.visual_cues: Bottom person on back with FEET ON THE BICEPS of the top person, pushing the arms. Legs are extended, creating distance. Often seen with gi grips on sleeves. Lasso: one leg wraps around the arm.
grapplemap_variations: spider guard w/ shins against biceps

closed_guard_top.name: Inside Closed Guard (Top)
closed_guard_top.visual_cues: Person kneeling or postured INSIDE the other's CLOSED (locked ankles) guard. Trapped between the bottom person's wrapped legs. Often hands on the bottom person's hips or chest trying to open the guard.
grapplemap_variations: half guard w/ underhook + wrist vs combat base
forward leaning leg drag vs t-rex

half_guard_top.name: Half Guard (Top)
half_guard_top.visual_cues: Top person is on top but has ONE LEG trapped by the bottom person. Upper body may be controlling with crossface/underhook. Trying to free the trapped leg to complete the pass.
grapplemap_variations: side half w/ lockdown, overhook and pimp-arm

open_guard_top.name: Passing Open Guard (Top)
open_guard_top.visual_cues: Top person standing or kneeling OVER the bottom person who is playing open guard. Trying to get past the legs. Gripping pants/ankles, stepping around feet. Not yet past the legs.

headquarters.name: Headquarters / Passing Position
headquarters.visual_cues: Top person has ONE KNEE between the bottom person's legs (splitting them) and the other knee outside. Stapling one leg down. Strong passing position with good base. Often pressing forward.
grapplemap_variations: knee slice vs de la riva
sliced guard vs cross grip and wrist pin
instructional_references: Ryan Hall "Arm Triangles", Volume 2: Creating Openings & Finishing, Technique 9: Half Guard Top: Brabo @ 1:01:53
Pablo Popovitch "NoGi Exposed", DVD 2: Guard Passing, Volume 10: Half Guard Series, Section 13: Knee Cross Pass @ 1:22:08
Pablo Popovitch "NoGi Exposed", DVD 1: Guard Passing, Volume 4: Cross Grip Series, Section 4: Knee Through Pass

side_control_top.name: Side Control (Top)
side_control_top.visual_cues: Top person is lying PERPENDICULAR (90 degrees) across the bottom person's chest. Chest-to-chest contact. NO LEGS between them — the pass is complete. Top person's hips are heavy on the mat. Often controlling with crossface and underhook.
grapplemap_variations: side control behind w/ double unders vs overhook
side control vs turned away t-rex
double unders side control vs headlock

mount_top.name: Mount (Top)
mount_top.visual_cues: Top person is SITTING ON the bottom person's torso/hips, STRADDLING them. Knees on both sides of the body. The bottom person is flat on their back underneath. Top person is upright or leaning forward.

back_mount.name: Back Mount / Back Control
back_mount.visual_cues: One person is BEHIND the other, chest-to-back. Usually has HOOKS IN (feet inside the thighs) or a body triangle (legs wrapped around the waist). The person in front cannot see the person behind them.
grapplemap_variations: seated back w/ hook on underhook side
mounted turtle w/ single hook & seatbelt
side control w/ seatbelt and first hook
back on choke arm side w/ one leg trapped

knee_on_belly.name: Knee on Belly
knee_on_belly.visual_cues: Top person has ONE KNEE planted on the bottom person's stomach/chest while the other foot is posted on the mat. The top person is essentially kneeling on the other. Very upright posture. One foot on the ground.
grapplemap_variations: KoB w/ collar tie and elbow

north_south_top.name: North-South (Top)
north_south_top.visual_cues: Top person is lying ON TOP but HEAD-TO-TOE (180 degrees). Both people's heads point in OPPOSITE directions. Top person's chest is on the bottom person's chest but they are facing opposite ways.

crucifix.name: Crucifix
crucifix.visual_cues: The top person has trapped BOTH of the bottom person's arms — one with the legs, one with the hands. The bottom person is stretched out like a cross with no arm defence. Often from the side or back.

side_control_bottom.name: Bottom Side Control
side_control_bottom.visual_cues: Person is flat on their BACK with the opponent lying across their chest (perpendicular). No legs between them. Being pinned and controlled. Often framing with forearms to create space.
grapplemap_variations: side control w/ crossface vs t-rex
judo side w/ head and near open elbow
side ctrl w/ near open elbow and crossface
side control w/ near bottom overhook and neck frame

mount_bottom.name: Bottom Mount
mount_bottom.visual_cues: Person is flat on their BACK with the opponent SITTING ON THEM, straddling their hips/torso. Being ridden. Arms are defending against chokes and armlocks.
grapplemap_variations: high mount w/ one arm trapped

back_taken.name: Back Taken (Defending)
back_taken.visual_cues: Person has the opponent BEHIND them on their back. They CANNOT SEE the attacker. Hooks or body triangle are in. Hands are fighting at the neck to defend chokes.

turtle_bottom.name: Turtle (Bottom)
turtle_bottom.visual_cues: Person is on their HANDS AND KNEES, curled up, face DOWN, protecting their neck. Looks like a ball or turtle shell. The opponent is on top or beside them trying to turn them or take the back.
grapplemap_variations: mounted turtle w/ double wrist control
turtle w/ open elbow vs vice
turtle w/ single-leg vs vice grip
coming up on posting turtle's leg

north_south_bottom.name: North-South (Bottom)
north_south_bottom.visual_cues: Person is pinned on their back with the opponent lying on top HEAD-TO-TOE. Being smothered. Heads point opposite directions.

single_leg_x.name: Single Leg X / Ashi Garami
single_leg_x.visual_cues: Bottom person is on their BACK with BOTH LEGS controlling ONE of the opponent's legs. One foot is on the hip, the other hooks behind the knee. The opponent's leg is between the attacker's legs. The opponent is usually still standing or kneeling.

straight_ashi.name: Straight Ashi Garami
straight_ashi.visual_cues: Attacker is on their back/side with the opponent's leg trapped between their legs. Both of attacker's legs are on the SAME SIDE of the opponent's trapped leg. The attacker's hips are next to the opponent's knee. Basic outside leg position.

outside_ashi.name: Outside Ashi Garami
outside_ashi.visual_cues: Similar to straight ashi but the attacker's OUTSIDE leg crosses OVER the opponent's body/hip. More control than straight ashi. The attacker's legs frame the opponent's knee from the outside.

inside_sankaku.name: Inside Sankaku (Honeyhole/Saddle)
inside_sankaku.visual_cues: Attacker has BOTH LEGS TRIANGLED (crossed/locked like a triangle) around the opponent's SINGLE LEG. The trapped leg goes BETWEEN the attacker's thighs. The attacker's legs form a triangle (figure-four) grip. This is the strongest heel hook position. The heel is typically being exposed/attacked.

outside_sankaku.name: Outside Sankaku (411)
outside_sankaku.visual_cues: Attacker has legs triangled around the opponent's leg but from the OUTSIDE. The attacker's body faces AWAY from the opponent (belly-down relative to the opponent's leg). The triangle grip is on the outside of the thigh. Used for outside heel hooks.

cross_ashi.name: Cross Ashi Garami
cross_ashi.visual_cues: Attacker controls the opponent's leg but their body is angled ACROSS — the attacker's legs cross to the opposite side of the opponent's body. A transitional position between inside and outside sankaku.

fifty_fifty.visual_cues: Both people have their legs EQUALLY entangled — each has one leg wrapped around the other's leg in a MIRROR image. Symmetrical position. Both people face the same direction with legs intertwined. Neither has a clear advantage.

backside_fifty_fifty.name: Backside 50/50
backside_fifty_fifty.visual_cues: Like 50/50 but one person has rotated so they face AWAY from the other (back-to-back relative to the entangled legs). One person faces the opponent's feet, the other faces away. Asymmetric advantage.

eighty_twenty.name: 80/20 (Dominant 50/50)
eighty_twenty.visual_cues: Looks like 50/50 but ONE player has their OUTSIDE LEG crossing OVER the opponent's hip/body, giving them dominant control. The dominant player's legs are higher on the opponent. Asymmetric — one player has significantly more control than the other.

ushiro_ashi.name: Ushiro Ashi Garami (Rear Ashi)
ushiro_ashi.visual_cues: Attacker is BEHIND the opponent's leg (facing the back of the knee). The attacker's body is oriented towards the opponent's back/rear. The trapped leg goes behind the attacker. Often used to attack the heel hook from a rear angle.

turtle_top.name: Turtle Attack (Top)
turtle_top.visual_cues: One person is on top of or beside the turtled person (who is on hands and knees). The attacker is draped over the turtle, reaching for the neck or trying to insert hooks. Often has a seatbelt grip (one arm over shoulder, one under armpit).
grapplemap_variations: double leg from knees vs sprawl

front_headlock.name: Front Headlock / Guillotine Position
front_headlock.visual_cues: One person has their arm wrapped around the OTHER person's HEAD/NECK from the front. The head is trapped under the armpit. Often the defender's head is down. Can be standing or on the ground. The controller's body is sprawled back.
instructional_references: Ryan Hall "Arm Triangles", Volume 3: Troubleshooting, Transitions and Drills, Technique 10: The Brabo Roll Under @ 46:35

dogfight.name: Dogfight / Underhook Battle
dogfight.visual_cues: Both people are on their KNEES facing each other, chest-to-chest, fighting for underhooks. Neither is clearly on top or bottom. A 50/50 wrestling position. Both trying to get underneath the other.

wrestling_scramble.name: Wrestling Scramble
wrestling_scramble.visual_cues: Fast movement, bodies tangled, neither person in a clear position. Someone may be shooting for a takedown, sprawling, or scrambling to their feet. Very dynamic and hard to freeze-frame.

guard_recovery.name: Guard Recovery (in progress)
guard_recovery.visual_cues: The bottom person is actively SHRIMPING (hip escaping) or turning to get their legs back in between. They were in a bad position and are creating space. Hip movement is visible, legs are coming back in.

pass_in_progress.name: Pass in Progress
pass_in_progress.visual_cues: The top person is MID-PASS — they are stepping or sliding around the bottom person's legs but haven't settled into side control yet. The legs are partially cleared but the position isn't consolidated.

sweep_in_progress.name: Sweep in Progress
sweep_in_progress.visual_cues: The bottom person is actively LIFTING or ROLLING the top person over. The top person is being off-balanced and may be mid-air or tilting. A reversal is happening in real-time.

back_exposure.name: Back Exposure (partially turned)
back_exposure.visual_cues: One person is partially turned away showing their BACK to the other but not fully in turtle or back control yet. Mid-transition — could go to back take or guard recovery. The exposed person's shoulders are rotating away.

submission_defence.name: Submission Defence (escaping)
submission_defence.visual_cues: One person is actively defending a submission — hands are fighting at the neck (defending choke), or stacking/posturing to escape an armbar, or rolling to relieve heel hook pressure. The defensive action is the primary thing happening.

scramble_generic.name: Scramble (generic)
scramble_generic.visual_cues: Cannot identify a specific position. Bodies are tangled in a way that doesn't match any defined position. Use this as a last resort.

unclear.name: Unclear / Cannot Determine
unclear.visual_cues: Frame is too blurry, dark, obstructed, or zoomed out to determine any position. Cannot see enough detail to classify.
